node.title: Need Guidance: DSA + Dev in 6–8 Months or Start Job Hunt Now?

article.title: Need Guidance: DSA + Dev in 6–8 Months or Start Job Hunt Now?
article.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m a 2025 ECE graduate with almost no programming exposure during college. I’m starting from scratch and feeling a bit lost on the right path to take.

Should I:

##### Spend the next 6–8 months learning DSA and development to build a strong foundation before applying for jobs, or

Focus on getting any job as soon as possible and learn skills along the way?

I’m really confused about what’s the smarter move for my future career. If you’ve been in a similar situation or have advice, I’d really appreciate your guidance on how to start and which direction to take.

Thanks in advance!

post.content:
Hi,

It depends on your personal situation.
If you have financial support from your parents, focus on learning DSA—it can skyrocket your career path in MAANG and Tier-1 product companies.

Service industries might hire you with just fundamental knowledge and aptitude, but growth will be slow.
For MAANG and Tier-1 product companies, interviews are going to be challenging—but never impossible.

So choose wisely based on your situation.

If you need suggestions on starting with DSA, please check this post:
https://leetcode.com/discuss/post/6992761/what-is-the-best-approach-to-learn-dsa-b-ub5u/comments/3090079

Remember, with DSA, start with a breadth-first approach—explore all the topics first and get a basic idea of each. Then move to the next topic or pattern.
You need to revise constantly to deepen your understanding.
Revision is the key when it comes to DSA and pattern recognition.

Excellent advice given by [@Dinesh Kumar](/u/DreamOn06)

My 2 cents: 

Getting work experience is extremely important and useful as you grow. Focus on doing the right things first (get any job, just start, get your hands dirty and then build iteratively), rather than trying to make a perfect career quickly. In mostly everyone's case, the "tomorrow" for studying never arrives! In that case, you at least would have a job at hand and you would start gaining some experience plus money is a plus. But you do have to have the discipline to keep studying to get to a better company to build you career. Otherwise, once you're in the boat at a low level company and get comfortable in that, its very difficult to transition, always need to remind yourself of the "why" and the "end goal".

Coming from an ECE background myself and then switching to computer science later in life, I had thought it is a monumental task that I have to compete with folks that our already in the computer science industry. However, it is just a matter of practice and consistency. 
You show up everyday, and nothing can be out of reach. I did the mistake of being scared of the difficult problems on Leetcode and feeling  "I'll never be able to solve these", but somehow still landed a FAANG job.

There comes a point, when you're just tired of being mediocre, so you just push all the limits, and then magic happens!

So don't be scared (= don't stress about how you'll land in your dream future), just focus on showing up today, rest will be taken care of!
